summary: Cotton quality reporting application for tracking and certifying 100-bale lots with quality metrics (Rd, trash, moisture, micronaire, length, strength)
architectural_notes: Single-page HTML application with vanilla JavaScript
Client-side data storage using array (no persistence)
Chart.js library included but not yet implemented
Responsive dashboard layout with 4-column stats grid
Print-specific CSS media queries for report generation
known_issues: No data persistence - all bale data lost on page refresh
Chart.js included but no charts implemented yet
No validation for reasonable value ranges on quality metrics
No ability to edit or delete entered bale records

existing_features.title: Implement a bale entry form with six numeric input fields: Rd, Trash %, Moisture %, Micronaire (Mic), Length (mm), and Strength (g/tex)
Display real-time statistics showing average Micronaire, average Length (mm), average Strength (g/tex), and average Trash % across all entered bales in stat cards
Render a sortable table displaying all entered bale records with columns for Bale #, Rd, Trash %, Moisture %, Mic, Length (mm), and Strength (g/tex), with newest bales appearing first

Display a header section showing 'COTTON QUALITY REPORT' title, lot reference '100-BALE-INSP-2026', current date, and lot status that changes from 'Incomplete' to 'COMPLETED' in green when 100 bales are entered
Implement print functionality that shows a clean report layout hiding the input form and print button when printing, with optimized styling for printed output

Apply a professional agricultural/industrial visual theme with clean dashboard layout featuring a neutral color palette (avoiding blue and purple), subtle borders, card-based statistics display, and clear data hierarchy suitable for quality inspection reporting
Verify all existing frontend components render correctly with the current backend actor implementation
Verify the frontend successfully connects to the backend actor using the generated declarations

new_feature_requests.summary: Bale data entry form with 6 quality metrics
Real-time calculation of average quality metrics across all bales
Dynamic table displaying all entered bale records
Print-friendly report layout with print button
Status tracking showing completion when 100 bales are entered
Create a Motoko backend actor to store and manage cotton bale records with fields: id (Nat), rd (Float), trash (Float), moist (Float), mic (Float), len (Float), and str (Float)
Ensure the Motoko backend actor compiles successfully and exposes all required public methods for bale management
